commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A focused automated repair can make the QQ Bot token SSRF exception host-scoped and update the existing token tests without choosing a new product direction.; Needs attention: The patch adds a justified SSRF exception, but the exception is broader than the host-scoped security claim because redirects inherit the policy.; Review finding: [P2] Scope the RFC2544 exception to the token host — extensions/qqbot/src... (sha=9bd9584000ffb060631f9f21f7d835eb5c41393a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an RFC2544 benchmark-range SSRF policy to the QQ Bot token fetch call and updates the token manager tests to expect that policy.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +17. Total +35 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and v2026.5.28 call fetchWithSsrFGuard without a policy, while the SDK blocks 198.18.0.0/15 unless allowRfc2544BenchmarkRange is set. I did not run a live QQ fake-IP setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused automated repair can make the QQ Bot token SSRF exception host-scoped and update the existing token tests without choosing a new product direction.
